bounty_title: Best 60s POIDH ad from Ep 19 w/ Kenny
judging_note: UPDATE 2026-05-26: real durations resolved via ffprobe on the downloaded MP4s (see poidh-round2-durations.json). Floor rule '45 to 60 seconds' now enforced - 3 strict PASS (6645, 6584, 6585), 2 NEAR-OVER (6586 at 60.2s, 6608 at 60.5s), 2 FAIL-OVER (6644 at 66.7s, 6616 at 91.9s). Rankings restructured accordingly.

floor_rules.label: Edited (cuts, not a raw screen recording)

rubric_tiers.label: Craft
rubric_tiers.examples: Tight cuts, captions, hook in first 3s, clear CTA, watchable on mute

rubric_tiers.label: Substance
rubric_tiers.examples: Hits the POIDH thesis from Ep 19: permissionless real-world coordination, not generic crypto hype

rubric_tiers.label: Bonus
rubric_tiers.examples: Adds creative spin, attributes the source episode by name, encourages new bounties

submissions.x_text: How do we pull money to get things done in the real world? On BCZ YapZ Ep 19, @kennyistyping explains how POIDH brings transparent, permissionless coordination to life

rubric_score.distribution: STRONG - tagged all three: @bettercallzaal, @kennyistyping, @poidhxyz. Links poidh.xyz.
rubric_score.craft: Clean copy, emoji hook, two-question setup landing the POIDH answer. Needs playback to confirm edit quality.
rubric_score.substance: Directly cites Ep 19 + Kenny by name. Hits the permissionless coordination thesis exactly.
rubric_score.bonus: Cross-tagged POIDH team itself + linked poidh.xyz.

why_pick: DURATION CONFIRMED 59.70s - inside the 45-60s window with 0.3s to spare
Only submission that names BCZ YapZ Ep 19 + @kennyistyping in copy - reads as a real attribution, not a generic ad
Full tag-set: @bettercallzaal + @kennyistyping + @poidhxyz, plus poidh.xyz link
9 likes = second highest engagement of cohort
Two-question copy framing ('how do we...?' -> 'on Ep 19, X explains...') is classic ad structure
Of the 3 clean-floor submissions, this has the strongest copy + tags + substance
why_skip: Posted 32 min before deadline - little time to gather engagement, currently 0 replies
Video resolution 480x270 (16:9, desktop crop) - less mobile-native than vertical formats
key_unknowns: Edit quality: thumbnail looks like a presentation slide; confirm tight cuts vs slide-show pacing
Does the audio hold up at 1x speed without subs?
submissions.if_video_lands: WINNER. Floor PASS + tags + Ep 19 cite + substance = unbeatable on strict reading.
submissions.if_video_doesnt: Even if edit quality is mediocre, this still wins on the strict 45-60s rule - the only strong candidate without a duration penalty.
submissions.claude_notes: RESOLVED 2026-05-26: 59.70s duration confirms floor PASS. Promoted to TOP PICK because it is the only clean-PASS submission with all three brand tags + an explicit Ep 19 citation in copy.
submissions.winner_pick_reason: Zaal picked Monksage 2026-05-27. The intro framing (How do you pull money to get things done in the real world? -> answer from Ep 19) is the strongest 3-second hook in the cohort. Video opens with blue title cards that match the copy's question.

submissions.x_text: We're never gonna have good adoption if all crypto does is gambling.

rubric_score.craft: Punchy one-liner with anti-gambling angle. Stops the scroll.
rubric_score.substance: Captures the 'real utility' frame from Ep 19 without naming POIDH explicitly in copy - the video has to make the POIDH connection.

why_pick: DURATION CONFIRMED 59.70s - clean PASS, identical to 6645
Strong contrarian hook: 'crypto's just gambling' is exactly Kenny's pushback frame from Ep 19
4 likes (third-highest of cohort, only 6608 at 11 and 6645 at 9 ahead)
Posted earliest of cohort (2026-05-14) - had 11 days to find audience
Quotable one-liner = screenshot-friendly copy
Promoted from FLOOR_PASS_WEAK to STRONG_CANDIDATE now that floor is cleanly passed
why_skip: Missing @poidhxyz tag - smaller distribution surface than 6645
Doesn't name POIDH or BCZ YapZ Ep 19 in copy at all - relies 100% on video
If video doesn't make the POIDH bridge, the submission reads as generic anti-gambling
0 replies despite 4 likes - viewers agreed but didn't engage further
key_unknowns: Does the video bridge 'crypto = gambling problem' to 'POIDH = real-world coordination answer'?
Is 4 likes the account's normal baseline or an outlier?
submissions.if_video_lands: Solid #2 pick. Punchy copy + clean duration + decent engagement = podium spot.
submissions.if_video_doesnt: Drops to 3rd or 4th. Copy alone doesn't carry if video misses POIDH.
submissions.claude_notes: RESOLVED 2026-05-26: 59.70s duration. Floor cleanly passed; promoted from weak-pass to strong candidate.

submissions.x_text: I think that permissionless aspect of anyone being able to put up a sufficient bounty...

rubric_score.craft: Cliffhanger copy (... ends mid-sentence). May intrigue or may frustrate scroll-by readers.
rubric_score.substance: On-thesis (permissionless bounty creation) but truncated - the video has to land the rest.

why_pick: DURATION CONFIRMED 57.49s - cleanest mid-range PASS of the cohort
On-thesis - 'permissionless aspect of anyone being able to put up a bounty' is Kenny's Ep 19 message verbatim
Got 1 reply = only weak-floor submission with any conversation
amplify_video format suggests polished upload, not screen-record
Promoted from FLOOR_PASS_WEAK to STRONG_CANDIDATE on duration confirmation
why_skip: Missing @poidhxyz tag - smaller surface
Cliffhanger copy is a coin-flip - works if video pays off, frustrates if it doesn't
2 likes + 1 reply after 11 days - low traction
Doesn't name BCZ YapZ Ep 19 or Kenny in copy
key_unknowns: What completes the cliffhanger sentence in the video?
Was the 1 reply positive or asking for clarification?
submissions.if_video_lands: Solid #3 pick. On-thesis + clean duration + amplify upload.
submissions.if_video_doesnt: Drops to 4th. Floor PASS but no rubric standout points.
submissions.claude_notes: RESOLVED 2026-05-26: 57.49s duration. Cleanest middle-of-range PASS. Promoted from weak-pass.

submissions.x_text: Crypto's hype is currently drowning out its actual help

rubric_score.craft: Single-line copy. Strength = punchline if video delivers. Weakness = no context.
rubric_score.substance: Strong thesis line but does not name POIDH in copy.

why_pick: Duration 60.21s - 0.21s over the 60s cap. Strict reading = FAIL, lenient reading = PASS
Strong contrarian hook line stops the scroll
why_skip: Floor rule says '45 to 60 seconds' - 60.21s is technically over
Copy doesn't name POIDH AT ALL
Missing @poidhxyz tag
Only 2 likes after 10 days
No CTA, no link, no thesis statement
key_unknowns: Zaal's call: is 0.21s over a forgivable rounding or a strict fail?
If accepted as PASS, would still rank below 6585 on rubric
submissions.if_video_lands: If Zaal allows ~0.5s tolerance, finishes 4th. Otherwise FAIL.
submissions.if_video_doesnt: FAIL on strict reading, mediocre rubric.
submissions.claude_notes: RESOLVED 2026-05-26: 60.21s. Crosses the 60.00s line by 0.21s. Demoted to BORDERLINE_DURATION.

submissions.x_text: this is POIDH. this is for POIDH. POIDH is one of the most active mini apps on Farcaster where you can join daily bounties or create your own for people to complete tasks, make content, and earn rewards.

rubric_score.distribution: STRONG - 4 tags including @farcaster_xyz. Cross-posted to Farcaster /poidh + /zao.
rubric_score.craft: Vertical 320x426 video = mobile-native, scroll-stopping. Repetition hook.
rubric_score.substance: Frames POIDH as 'most active mini app' - true but off Kenny's real-world coordination thesis.
rubric_score.bonus: Highest engagement of cohort (11 likes) + cross-posted to Farcaster channels.

why_pick: Duration 60.46s - 0.46s over the cap. Strict reading = FAIL, lenient reading = PASS
Highest engagement of all 8 submissions (11 likes)
Only vertical (320x426) format - mobile-native, fits a phone screen
Most tags (4) including @farcaster_xyz - widest distribution surface
Cross-posted to Farcaster /poidh + /zao per POIDH description
why_skip: Floor rule '45 to 60 seconds' - 60.46s is technically over (less forgivable than 6586's 0.21s)
Frames POIDH as 'most active mini app on Farcaster' - NOT Kenny's real-world coordination thesis
No mention of BCZ YapZ Ep 19 or Kenny by name in copy
Vertical format may not play well if you re-share to desktop
key_unknowns: Zaal's call: 0.46s over = forgivable or strict fail?
Strict scoring picks against Dee here even though she has best distro
submissions.if_video_lands: If Zaal allows ~0.5s tolerance, this is a STRONG candidate - best distro, highest engagement. If strict, FAIL.
submissions.if_video_doesnt: FAIL on strict reading. Even on lenient reading, the off-thesis copy hurts it.
submissions.claude_notes: RESOLVED 2026-05-26: 60.46s. Borderline. Lost 'best-ad-that-performed' lead because the strict 60s line is the bar. Dee's submission was the cohort's most engagement-savvy but the spec was 60s and she went 0.46s over.

submissions.x_text: Coordinate funds for laser-specific real-world tasks without relying on a single middleman. POIDH changes the game, permissionless bounty structures into action. All you need is a wallet. Post your bounty now at poidh.xyz

rubric_score.distribution: STRONG (rubric only) - all three tags + link to poidh.xyz + Farcaster cross-post.

why_pick: Rubric was top-tier - copy + tags + CTA were all on-target
Could be a Round 3 / honorable mention if Zaal wants to send a consolation cast
why_skip: Duration 66.71s - 6.71s over the 60s cap. Not a rounding issue, a clear over-shoot
The bounty floor literally says 'do these or you're not in the running'
If we let 66s pass, no rule applies to anyone else either
key_unknowns: Could submitter trim and resubmit on a future bounty? Yes - rubric quality is real, the rule got them
submissions.if_video_lands: Floor FAIL is binary - video quality doesn't override the spec.
submissions.claude_notes: RESOLVED 2026-05-26: 66.71s. Drops from STRONG_CANDIDATE to FLOOR_FAIL_DURATION. Hard but the bar is the bar. Send a kind 'rubric was strong, duration missed - aim for Round 3' note.

submissions.x_text: From struggling with healthcare coordination to realizing anyone can pool money for real-world tasks like cleaning up trash in Seattle - permissionless, with proof required. Pics Or It Didn't Happen.

rubric_score.craft: Best substance copy of the cohort - specific Seattle / healthcare examples.
rubric_score.substance: Pulls SPECIFIC story arcs from Ep 19 - the only one to do so this concretely.

why_pick: Cohort's best substance write-up - SPECIFIC Ep 19 references (Seattle trash, healthcare)
Demonstrates the submitter actually watched the episode
STRONG candidate for a 'best substance' honorable mention or invited to Round 3 with the same copy + trimmed video
why_skip: Duration 91.88s - 31.88s over the cap. Roughly 1.5x the spec
Cannot rationalize this as a tolerance issue
Floor rule is binary; great copy doesn't override it
key_unknowns: Was the 60s rule misread? Will the submitter trim and want a Round 3 entry?
submissions.if_video_lands: Doesn't matter - duration is binary FAIL.
submissions.claude_notes: RESOLVED 2026-05-26: 91.88s. Painful - this had my best substance score. Goes from 9/10 to FLOOR_FAIL_DURATION. The lesson for Round 3 is: surface duration check publicly so submitters self-check before posting.

rubric_score.substance: Description is generic POIDH boilerplate, not Ep 19 specific.

why_pick: n/a - no winning case. Floor fail by submission rules.
why_skip: Bounty rule 3 says 'Submit the X post URL on POIDH at this bounty page' - this submission has only text
No video to evaluate - cannot score any rubric category
Generic POIDH boilerplate, not Ep 19 specific

pick_decision_matrix.if_strict_floor_reading_60s_max: 6645 (Monksage) - only strict-PASS submission with all 3 brand tags + Ep 19 + Kenny named in copy
pick_decision_matrix.if_lenient_floor_tolerance_half_second: Still 6645 - 6608 (Dee) gets back in but Monksage's substance + tags win
pick_decision_matrix.if_substance_overrides_rule: 6616 (Ebuka) - rule-broken but copy unmatched. NOT recommended - sets a precedent that floor isn't real
pick_decision_matrix.if_distribution_overrides_rule: 6608 (Dee) - 11 likes + vertical + 4 tags but 0.46s over the cap

claude_summary.winner_pick_reason: Zaal's call (2026-05-27): Monksage's intro framing was the most enjoyable - the question-then-Ep-19-answer setup (How do you pull money to get things done in the real world? On BCZ YapZ Ep 19 @kennyistyping explains how POIDH brings transparent permissionless coordination to life) reads as a real ad. Plus 59.70s strict-floor pass + all 3 brand tags + poidh.xyz link + 9 likes.
claude_summary.recommended_winner_reason: After ffprobe-confirmed durations, 6645 (Monksage) is the only submission that (a) cleanly clears the 45-60s floor at 59.70s, (b) has the full @bettercallzaal + @kennyistyping + @poidhxyz tag-set, (c) names BCZ YapZ Ep 19 + Kenny in the copy itself, (d) sits in the top 2 for X engagement. Other strong contenders (6616, 6644, 6608) all broke the duration rule by a margin large enough to disqualify under a fair strict reading.
claude_summary.rejection_template_for_6634: Round 2 required submitting an X post URL per rule 3. Resubmit on Round 3 with the video link.
claude_summary.honorable_mention_template_for_6616: Your copy was the cohort's strongest - specific Ep 19 references (Seattle, healthcare). Duration ran 91s vs 60s cap. Trim and submit to Round 3 - you'd be a frontrunner.
claude_summary.honorable_mention_template_for_6644: Strong CTA copy + full tags. Came in at 66.71s vs 60s cap. Trim ~7s and you're a Round 3 finalist.

round_1_outcome.note: Round 1 (bounty 1151) winner confirmed accepted on-chain as of 2026-05-26 check. The same wallet then submitted to Round 2 (1166) without a video link, floor-failing claim 6634.
